name: Big Raviolis Stuffed with Mushrooms, Potatoes, Egg with Spi
recipeIngredient: 1 baking potato, peeled and cut into 6 pieces
1/4 pound Mascarpone cheese or sour cream
1/2 pound mixture of domestic and wild mushrooms, finely chopped
16 fresh thin pasta sheets, 5 x 5 inches
8 ripe plum tomatoes, peeled, seeded and cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch the garlic three times in salted boiling water. Add the potato pieces to a small pot with the blanched garlic and cover with water. Add a pinch of salt and cook until tender. Drain and press through a ricer while still warm.
2. Beat the Mascarpone or sour cream into the potatoes, seasoning with salt and pepper. Chop one shallot finely. Heat four tablespoons butter in a large frying pan, add the shallot and cook for 2 minutes. Add the mushrooms and cook until tender and dry, seasoning with salt and pepper. Mix the mushrooms with the potatoes. Add the chopped parsley and thyme. Allow to cool.
3. Beat one egg with one tablespoon water to form an egg wash. Brush the edges of a pasta sheet with wash. Place two tablespoons of the mushroom mixture in the center of the pasta. Make a well in the mixture. Crack an egg into a teacup and pour the yolk and half the white into the well, seasoning with salt and pepper. Cover with a second sheet of pasta and push out as much air as possible. Repeat with the remaining ravioli.
4. Thinly slice two shallots. Heat the remaining butter in a small pan. When the foam subsides, add the shallots, season with salt and pepper, and cook until tender. Add the hot red pepper flakes, tomatoes, and basil and cook for 2 minutes. Season with salt. Keep warm.
5. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Arrange Pancetta slices on a sheet pan and bake until they just begin to brown and crisp. Keep warm.
6. Bring five quarts of water to a boil and season with salt. Add the raviolis, stirring gently several times, and cook for 5 minutes, or until done; the yolks should still be runny. Scoop the raviolis out with a slotted spoon, drain, and place two on each warm plate.
7. Distribute the tomatoes over the raviolis and top with Pancetta slices. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese. If the plates have cooled, return them to a hot oven for 1 minute before serving. Garnish with parsley sprigs and serve immediately. Yield: 4 servings
